mission.title: Install and Configure Aeraki Mesh on Kubernetes
mission.description: Aeraki is a service mesh that allows you to manage any layer-7 protocols, enhancing the capabilities of microservices. Installing Aeraki enables you to handle diverse traffic types beyond just HTTP, making your service mesh more versatile.

steps.title: Install Istio (prerequisite)
steps.description:
Aeraki Mesh extends Istio. Install Istio first:
```bash
curl -L https://istio.io/downloadIstio | ISTIO_VERSION=1.21.0 sh -
export PATH=$PWD/istio-1.21.0/bin:$PATH
istioctl install --set profile=demo -y
```

steps.title: Install Aeraki Mesh
steps.description:
Clone the Aeraki repository and run the install script:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/aeraki-framework/aeraki.git
cd aeraki
bash demo/install-aeraki.sh
```
This installs the Aeraki controller and MetaProtocol CRDs.

steps.title: Deploy a sample application
steps.description:
Test with the MetaProtocol Dubbo sample:
```bash
bash demo/install-demo.sh
kubectl get pods -n meta-dubbo
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n istio-system | grep aeraki
kubectl get crd | grep aeraki
kubectl get metarouter -A
```

uninstall.title: Remove Aeraki
uninstall.description:
```bash
kubectl delete -f demo/
kubectl delete namespace meta-dubbo
```

upgrade.title: Pull latest and reinstall
upgrade.description:
```bash
cd aeraki && git pull
bash demo/install-aeraki.sh
```

troubleshooting.title: Aeraki controller not starting
troubleshooting.description:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app=aeraki -n istio-system
```
Ensure Istio is installed and running first.

troubleshooting.title: MetaRouter not taking effect
troubleshooting.description:
Verify the MetaRouter CRD exists:
```bash
kubectl get crd | grep aeraki
```

troubleshooting.title: Istio version incompatibility
troubleshooting.description: Check the Aeraki compatibility matrix at https://github.com/aeraki-framework/aeraki#compatibility

resolution.summary: Aeraki Mesh is installed alongside Istio for managing non-HTTP protocols like Dubbo, Thrift, and Redis.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and kubectl installed on your local machine.
